30 - Rishabh Pant

21 - Shikhar Dhawan

NEW DELHI: Shreyas Iyer marked his return to India’s T20I side in historic fashion on Friday, taking charge of the team on his captaincy debut in the format after the longest absence from T20Is before leading a side internationally.When Iyer walked out for the toss in the opening T20I against Ireland in Belfast, he had missed 63 consecutive T20 internationals for India since his previous appearance against Australia in Bengaluru on December 3, 2023. The remarkable gap is the longest by any player before captaining their national side in T20Is for the first time.The 31-year-old surpassed New Zealand’s Tom Latham, who made his T20I captaincy debut after missing 47 consecutive matches between 2017 and 2021. Australia’s Rachel Haynes is third on the list after a 40-match gap between 2013 and 2017.Iyer's previous T20I appearance came in India's six-run victory over Australia, where he top-scored with a fluent 53 off 37 deliveries, striking five fours and two sixes.Iyer's return also saw him add two more unique records to his name.At 31 years and 202 days, he became the third-oldest player to make his T20I captaincy debut for India, behind only Shikhar Dhawan (35 years, 232 days) and Suryakumar Yadav (33 years, 70 days). Rohit Sharma and KL Rahul complete the top five.The Mumbai batter also became the most experienced T20 captain before making his India captaincy debut in the format. Having led 114 T20 matches across franchise and domestic cricket before Friday's game, he surpassed Rohit Sharma (80), Virat Kohli (72), KL Rahul (42) and Rishabh Pant (30).Winning the toss, Iyer opted to field first on an unfamiliar surface in Belfast."We're going to field first. It's a new surface for us, and it's the first time the majority of us have played over here. Considering the conditions, the environment and the wicket, we decided to field first," he said.One of the biggest talking points before the match was whether 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi would make his international debut and become India's youngest men's international cricketer.However, Iyer confirmed the teenager would have to wait."Unfortunately, no," he said when asked about Sooryavanshi's inclusion.The skipper explained that team balance dictated the decision while assuring the youngster that his opportunity would come.
Article Summary: Shreyas Iyer captained India in opening T20I against Ireland in Belfast. 31-year-old had missed 63 consecutive T20 internationals for India. Iyer became the third-oldest player to make his T 20I captaincy debut for India, behind only Shikhar Dhawan and Suryakumar Yadav.

Article Title: 'Can't believe it': Ex-England captain stunned by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi snub
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Former England captain Michael Vaughan expressed his surprise after India left teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi out of the playing XI for the opening T20I against Ireland despite the youngster's impressive rise in recent months.Sooryavanshi, who recently earned his maiden India call-up, was widely tipped to make his international debut in Belfast. However, India opted to retain the experienced opening combination of Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma , meaning the 15-year-old had to wait for his opportunity.Soon after India's playing XI was announced and before the match got underway, Vaughan questioned the decision on social media."Can’t believe India haven’t picked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi .. the best T20 player at the moment in the World," Vaughan tweeted.India captain Shreyas Iyer won the toss and elected to field first, with Harshit Rana leading an impressive bowling effort. The fast bowler, returning after a four-month injury lay-off, claimed 3/24 as India reduced Ireland to 36/3 inside the Powerplay.Despite the early setbacks, Ireland recovered through captain Lorcan Tucker, who struck a composed 50 off 36 balls.Gareth Delany then blasted 49, while George Dockrell added a quickfire 19 as Ireland finished on a competitive 182/9. A 27-run over off Prasidh Krishna in the death overs proved crucial in lifting the hosts to a challenging total.India's chase never truly gathered momentum. Abhishek Sharma produced a blazing 20-ball half-century, but Ireland's disciplined pace attack kept striking at regular intervals. Debutant Jai Moondra dismissed Sanju Samson early, while Matt Hollard removed both Ishan Kishan and Shreyas Iyer to put India on the back foot.With Tilak Varma, Shivam Dube and the lower order unable to build meaningful partnerships, India were bowled out for 148 as Ireland sealed a historic 34-run victory, their first-ever win over India in any format.While the result sparked plenty of discussion around India's batting approach, Vaughan's pre-match remarks on Sooryavanshi's omission also drew significant attention as the teenager continued to wait for his international debut.
Article Summary: India left teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi out of the playing XI for the opening T20I against Ireland. Former England captain Michael Vaughan questioned the decision on social media. India were bowled out for 148 as Ireland sealed a historic 34-run victory, their first-ever win over India in any format.

Article Title: 'A series win over India would be pretty special': Ireland captain Lorcan Tucker fires warning after historic Belfast triumph
Article Text:
Ireland captain Lorcan Tucker celebrates the historic win with teammates. (Pic credit: Cricket Ireland)

'We scrapped through and stayed in the game'



ALSO READ:

Debutants earn captain's praise



NEW DELHI: Ireland captain Lorcan Tucker has set his sights on an even bigger prize after guiding his side to a historic first-ever victory over India in men's international cricket, declaring that sealing a series win over the reigning T20 world champions would be "pretty special. "Fresh from Ireland's stunning 34-run victory in the opening T20I in Belfast on Friday, Tucker was careful not to get carried away but admitted the opportunity to wrap up a famous series triumph at home had already ignited the dressing room."Absolutely, yeah. A series win at home against India would be pretty special," Tucker said after the match. "I think the lads are absolutely keen to get back here. The energy today was fabulous, the crowd were brilliant, so special."Tucker described the victory as one of the finest moments in Irish cricket, especially considering it came against the two-time defending T20 World Cup champions."It was pretty special. Credit to the lads," he said. "We scrapped through that game. We had some tough periods in it, but we stayed in it. We were diligent, we worked hard, and we were lucky to get the reward in the end."Ireland looked in trouble at 36 for 3 after India's seamers made full use of the conditions, before Tucker steadied the innings with a composed 50 from 36 deliveries.Reflecting on his crucial partnership with Gareth Delany, who blasted 49, Tucker praised India's disciplined bowling before explaining how Ireland rebuilt patiently."India bowled really well at the start. They stuck to their lengths and didn't really waver. It almost felt like a Test match at one stage. But we tried to stay in the game, build a partnership, and then take our opportunities when they came."Tucker also reserved special praise for debutants Jai Moondra and Matt Hollard, whose combined five wickets dismantled India's batting line-up."I think we learned a lot from how India bowled. We told the lads to stick to those lengths as well, and they showed their skill, accuracy and ability to execute the plans. Absolute credit to them."He added that the result reflected years of hard work behind the scenes."To beat the world champions at home is pretty special. The two debutants were outstanding, but it's also a credit to everyone behind the scenes at Cricket Ireland. Everyone works so hard, and results like this are why we do it."India, led by new captain Shreyas Iyer, were bowled out for 148 after Ireland posted 182 for 9, leaving Tucker and his side just one win away from a landmark series triumph over one of world cricket's biggest powers.
Article Summary: Ireland captain Lorcan Tucker says series win over India would be 'pretty special' Ireland beat India by 34 runs in opening T20I in Belfast on Friday. Tucker praised debutants Jai Moondra and Matt Hollard, who took five wickets each.

Article Title: Shreyas Iyer joins Virat Kohli, Rishabh Pant and Shubman Gill on unwanted list after Ireland script historic upset
Article Text:
Shreyas Iyer (Pic credit: BCCI)

Indian captains losing their maiden T20I in charge

Virat Kohli vs England in Kanpur 2017

Rishabh Pant vs South Africa in Delhi 2022

Shubman Gill vs Zimbabwe in Harare 2024

Shreyas Iyer vs Ireland in Belfast 2026

Historic return overshadowed by historic defeat



Ireland's debutants steal the show



NEW DELHI: Shreyas Iyer's first outing as India's T20I captain ended on a disappointing note on Friday as Ireland stunned the reigning world champions by 34 runs in Belfast, consigning the Mumbai batter to an unwanted place in the record books.With the defeat, Iyer became only the fourth Indian captain to lose his maiden T20I in charge, joining an unfortunate list that includes Virat Kohli, Rishabh Pant and Shubman Gill.Kohli's first match as T20I captain ended in defeat against England at Kanpur in 2017. Pant suffered the same fate against South Africa in Delhi in 2022, while Gill's captaincy debut ended with a loss to Zimbabwe in Harare in 2024. Iyer has now added his name to that list following Ireland's historic victory.The result also marked Ireland's first-ever win over India in men's international cricket.Ironically, Iyer had entered the match with a remarkable record already to his name.The 31-year-old returned to India's T20I side after missing 63 consecutive matches since his previous appearance against Australia in December 2023 — the longest gap by any player before captaining a national team in T20 internationals.His captaincy debut also brought two more milestones. At 31 years and 202 days, he became the third-oldest player to make his T20I captaincy debut for India, behind Shikhar Dhawan and Suryakumar Yadav.Iyer also became the most experienced T20 captain before making his India captaincy debut, having led 114 T20 matches across franchise and domestic cricket, ahead of Rohit Sharma (80), Virat Kohli (72) and KL Rahul (42).However, the records counted for little once the action began.Asked to bat first, Ireland recovered brilliantly from 51 for 4 to post 182 for 9, thanks to captain Lorcan Tucker's fluent 50 and Gareth Delany's 49.India's chase never gained momentum despite Abhishek Sharma's explosive 50. Ireland's debutants proved to be the difference as India-born left-arm pacer Jai Moondra claimed 2 for 25, while Matt Hollard impressed with 3 for 28 to trigger a batting collapse.India were eventually bowled out for 148 with seven balls to spare, handing Ireland one of the greatest victories in their cricket history.
Article Summary: Shreyas Iyer's first outing as India's T20I captain ended on a disappointing note. Ireland stunned the reigning world champions by 34 runs in Belfast. Iyer became only the fourth Indian captain to lose his maiden T20i in charge. Virat Kohli, Rishabh Pant and Shubman Gill also lost their first matches.

Article Title: 'You can't just turn up and win': Shreyas Iyer's blunt message after Ireland shocker
Article Text:
Shreyas Iyer (X-BCCI)

India captain Shreyas Iyer admitted his side let the game slip despite making the ideal start with the ball as Ireland stunned the reigning T20 world champions by 34 runs in the opening T20I to take a 1-0 lead in the two-match series.Leading India for the first time as full-time T20I captain, Iyer felt the bowlers executed their plans well in the early stages but failed to maintain the same discipline during the middle and death overs, allowing Ireland to recover from a precarious position."I think initially, the bowlers were bowling well. They got lateral movement, and they got a couple of wickets as well. So we got a tremendous start, but in between, we lost execution. We made them hit straight down the ground, where we know that the dimensions are pretty small. But then, yeah, the bowlers, when we had to shut down the overs, they were brilliant as well. So I thought 140 would have been an amazing score considering the start that we got to chase. But nonetheless, it’s a great experience over here. We got to play in these conditions, got a fair idea about the wicket, and yeah, great start as a captain."After reducing Ireland to 36/3 in the Powerplay, India allowed Lorcan Tucker, Gareth Delany and George Dockrell to turn the innings around as the hosts eventually posted 182/9. Delany and Dockrell's assault on Prasidh Krishna in the death overs, including a 27-run over, proved decisive.Asked whether India's long break had contributed to the defeat, Iyer acknowledged there was rust but insisted the team would quickly move on and focus on the series decider."Yes, definitely. We’re going to forget what’s happened. Definitely a lot to learn from this game and see to it that we come out in the next game all guns blazing."The India skipper reserved special praise for Harshit Rana, who impressed with figures of 3/24 in his return from injury, while also highlighting the value of Shivam Dube's contribution with the ball."Yes, certainly. They were getting help up front at the start, and Shivam, he has bowled crucial overs in the past. So I always back him, and I’ve played with him in the domestic circuit as well. I know his strengths and weaknesses. And Harshit Rana, he bowled phenomenal. Coming out of an injury to perform at this level and showcase this sort of talent, it’s simply brilliant."India's chase never gathered momentum despite Abhishek Sharma 's explosive 20-ball fifty, with the visitors eventually bowled out for 148 as Ireland registered their first-ever win over India in any format.Reflecting on the defeat, Iyer urged his teammates not to become complacent and stressed the importance of capitalising whenever an opponent is under pressure."I think you can’t take anything for granted. You can’t just turn up and win matches. You have to work hard and see to it that you’re in that moment. Like I mentioned earlier, you’ve got to stay in the present and see to it that if you’ve got that opportunity to squeeze the opponent, we’ve got to do that. And yeah, never take any moment or situation lightly."Despite the disappointing result in his first match as India's permanent T20I captain, Iyer made it clear the team is already focused on bouncing back."Yes, looking forward to it."
Article Summary: India captain Shreyas Iyer admits his side let the game slip. Ireland stunned the reigning T20 world champions by 34 runs in the opening T20I to take a 1-0 lead in the two-match series. Iyer felt the bowlers executed their plans well in the early stages but failed to maintain the same discipline during the middle and death overs.

Article Title: Australia remain just as strong even without Alyssa Healy, says Harmanpreet ahead of must-win T20 World Cup clash
Article Text:
Photo/Agencies

Harmanpreet Kaur Dances with Young Dhol Player Fan

India captain Harmanpreet Kaur has said her team will focus on its own game rather than Australia's record when the two sides meet in what could decide India's place in the semi-finals of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026.India face Australia at Lord's in their final Group A match, knowing a win will improve their chances of reaching the last four.Speaking on JioStar, Harmanpreet said Australia remain one of the strongest teams despite the retirement of Alyssa Healy."We recently played a T20I series against Australia on their home soil, and we performed well. That series gave us confidence. It's not that after Alyssa Healy's retirement, Australia have become weaker. They are still a very strong side. They have a deep pool of players and a system that keeps producing quality cricketers. One player leaving doesn't change that," said Kaur.Australia, led by all-rounder Sophie Molineux , have won all four of their matches so far in the tournament. Another victory against India will confirm their place in the semi-finals.Harmanpreet praised Molineux's leadership, saying, "Sophie Molineux is a great captain too.She leads from the front and knows how to get the best out of her players. She is calm under pressure and reads the game well. She has been part of this Australian setup for a long time, so she understands the dynamics of the team. Under her leadership, they will be well prepared. But we have always played good cricket against them. "She added, "We have always given them tough competition in big tournaments. The mindset remains the same; we will give our best, work hard, and try to dominate them in every department. We know they are a strong team, but we also know we have the ability to match them."The India captain said her team is concentrating on its own plans rather than spending time thinking about Australia's strengths."We know Australia have great cricketers. They have world-class players in every department. But we don't spend too much time thinking about them. We focus on our own skills. We talk about what we need to do on the match day, our batting plans, our bowling lengths, and our fielding standards. We discuss how we can execute our skills under pressure," she said.She concluded, "We know what Australia have achieved in the past, but that doesn't matter when we step onto the field. It's only about what we can do and how we can deliver to win that match. So, we keep the focus on ourselves. We talk about our strengths and how we can use them against them. That is what we will continue to do."
Article Summary: India face Australia at Lord's in their final Group A match. A win will improve their chances of reaching the last four. Australia have won all four of their matches so far in the tournament. Another victory against India will confirm their place in the semi-finals.

Article Title: Will World Cup witness another 'disgrace of gijon? Austria vs Algeria raises fears
Article Text:
Why Austria vs Algeria Is Raising Similar Fears (Image: X)

How could this situation have been avoided?

Will the modern World Cup’s chapter of infamy repeat itself ? That’s what has got everyone worried as Austria prepares to take on Algeria in the last set of group games. The final Group J games will be played on Sunday, 7.30 am IST. Argentina’s meeting with Jordan in Dallas will be about Lionel Scaloni trying out his reserve players in attack and midfield as the defending champions assess their options for the knockouts. While already-eliminated Jordan will play for pride, it will also be about Messi’s goal count.But when Austria and Algeria take the field in Kansas, both teams will know what the cut-off for qualification for the third-placed team is going to be. Eight best-placed third-placed teams will make the Round of 32 and by the looks of it, four points earned by the third-placed team is likely to be sufficient for qualification. In the case of, say, nine teams finding themselves with four points each, they will then be decided on goal difference.Do bear in mind, however that, in 1994, current-day powerhouses Norway were eliminated despite sitting with four points but finding themselves bottom of their group.As things stand, Algeria and Austria sit on three points each and they will know exactly if they will need just one point to qualify as Nos. 2 and 3 teams from the group, since all the other teams would have finished their engagements. Unless there are already eight third placed teams of the 11 earlier groups with four points each, there’s no reason for Austria and Algeria — two of the three protagonists of the infamous ‘Disgrace of Gijon’ — to go all out in search of victory. For context, the ‘Disgrace of Gijon’ occured at the 1982 World Cup, a result of connivance which led FIFA to bring in the rule that the final games of a group must be played simultaneously.In Gijon, West Germany and Austria played out a 1-0 win in favour of the fourtime champions to ensure that both the teams qualify from the group. It ensured Algeria’s ouster, who were on same points as the two European teams and had finished their group engagements before. Seeing what was happening, some commentators refused to talk about the match and asked viewers to switch off their TVs. A local newspaper even published the game’s scoreline under the crime section.But now, Algeria — the aggrieved party 44 years ago — sit on the cusp of a probable ‘Disgrace of Kansas City,’ something that world football doesn’t need.With the games in full flow now, there’s not much FIFA can do about it. At best, they can threaten to sanction the teams if foul-play can be proved. That said, FIFA will have a tough time trying to show that the teams didn’t give their best to force a result. Going forward, to eliminate this conundrum, there could be a 64-team field. As bloated as that will be, there will be at least 16 groups with two qualifying from each group, which avoids this tricky thirdplace issue.Or simply, FIFA reverts to the tried and tested method of goal difference. Another option is to have games of all the teams in the final round at the same time. It will mean 24 matches being playing at the same time, something close to the way the 32-team Champions League group-stage is played currently.In a World Cup situation, this could mean a logistical and organisational nightmare. This year, the tournament is being played at 16 venues across three nations. In 2030, the hosts will be Spain, Morocco and Uruguay and FIFA will need to figure out how they will be able to carry out such a mammoth task if the matches are spread across 24 venues.For now, the world will hope that Algeria and Austria maintain the spirit of the sport and make it grand end to a most entertaining group stage.
Article Summary: Austria and Algeria sit on three points each and will know exactly if they will need just one point to qualify as Nos. 2 and 3 teams from the group. The ‘Disgrace of Gijon’ occured at the 1982 World Cup, a result of connivance which led FIFA to bring in the rule that the final games of a group must be played simultaneously.

Article Title: Yuto Nagatomo scripts history as first Asian player to play in five FIFA World Cups
Article Text:
n a groundbreaking achievement, Yuto Nagatomo has made history as the first Asian footballer to play in five FIFA World Cups. At 39, the Japanese defender's extraordinary career began in 2010, culminating in his appearance at the 2026 tournament. (Photo/ANI)

A journey of pure defiance

Japanese football legend Yuto Nagatomo etched his name into the halls of absolute immortality, becoming the first Asian player in football history to participate in five different editions of the FIFA World Cup The tireless 39-year-old full-back achieved the extraordinary milestone when he stepped onto the pitch during Japan's high-octane group-stage encounter at the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026. By doing so, Nagatomo broke away from a select group of continental icons to claim an exclusive piece of Asian football history.Nagatomo’s incredible World Cup odyssey began sixteen years ago at the 2010 edition in South Africa, where his explosive energy down the flanks caught the global eye. He remained an indispensable, driving force for the Samurai Blue through the tournaments in Brazil (2014), Russia (2018), Qatar (2022), and now the historic 2026 edition across North America.Renowned for his ferocious work ethic and elite physical conditioning, the former Inter Milan defender has defied time, sports science, and generations of younger challengers to retain his spot in the national setup. His inclusion is not merely sentimental; Nagatomo’s immense dressing-room presence and tactical discipline have been vital anchors for a flying Japanese squad that has taken the 2026 tournament by storm.With his fifth World Cup appearance, Nagatomo joins a legendary, ultra-exclusive global club of football icons who have achieved the feat, standing alongside the likes of Cristiano Ronaldo Lionel Messi , Lothar Matthäus, and Rafael Márquez.For Asian football, his longevity sets a monumental benchmark, proving that continental athletes can sustain peak performance at the absolute summit of international football for nearly two decades. As Japan transitions into the knockout rounds with grand ambitions, their timeless veteran remains the spiritual heartbeat of the squad, showing the world that the fire of the Samurai Blue refuses to burn out.
Article Summary: Yuto Nagatomo is the first Asian footballer to play in five FIFA World Cups. The 39-year-old defender made his debut at the 2010 World Cup in South Africa. He has since played in the 2014, 2018 and 2026 editions of the tournament.

Article Title: Why top managers are ditching group top spots for player longevity at the World Cup 2026
Article Text:
Tournament strategies are evolving as managers prioritize long-term success over immediate group standings. Norway's Stale Solbakken gambled on rest, sacrificing a match to prepare for crucial knockout stages, a move he deemed "a no-brainer." (AP Photo)

"A complete no-brainer"

Calculated risks vs. momentum

Experience changes everything. In his younger days, Portugal manager Roberto Martínez would have stressed over finishing first in his World Cup group.“You sit down and you are so inexperienced, you want to plan everything,” Martinez admitted. Now managing in his third tournament, he has learned that ultimate plans rarely survive reality. He is far from alone in that realization.Norway manager Stale Solbakken put theory into practice on Saturday, deliberately prioritizing rest and load management over an all-out assault to win Group I against tournament favorites France. Sitting out superstar striker Erling Haaland alongside nine other regular starters, a depleted Norway side suffered a heavy 4-1 defeat. Yet, with a crucial Round of 32 clash looming against Ivory Coast on Tuesday, Solbakken expressed zero coaching regrets.“The break that we had from the last game to this game is the shortest of any team, and we have another match in just a couple of days, so it’s a no-brainer,” Solbakken explained. “Fans around Norway and also in the arena would have liked to see Erling, but that is not really the issue here.We want to proceed as long as we can here in the tournament.”The tactical defeat sends Norway to Arlington, Texas, to play Ivory Coast. The winner of that tie will move on to the Round of 16 to meet either Brazil or Japan. Meanwhile, an undefeated France team, buoyed by a clinical first-half hat-trick from Ousmane Dembele, heads to East Rutherford, New Jersey, to face a likely opponent in Sweden, with a potential heavyweight block against Germany waiting down the line.France assistant coach Guy Stéphan, standing in for Didier Deschamps following the passing of Deschamps' mother, wasn't caught off guard by Norway's sweeping lineup changes. “We will see the result in four days,” Stéphan remarked.Other elite tacticians are weighing their strategies differently. Martinez wants his Portugal side to maintain a perfect winning habit ahead of their Group K closer against Colombia, even though Portugal's ticket to the Round of 32 is virtually secured.“I believe that the focus should be trying to win every game, trying to create the best possible atmosphere in your dressing room, forget about the opponent,” Martinez stated. “Respect the opponent, but if you want to do well in the tournament, you have to be able to beat everybody and anybody.”Colombia coach Nestor Lorenzo remains wary of calculating exact routes, warning that trying to manipulate tables can backfire. Meanwhile, Spain boss Luis de la Fuente has completely ignored the bracket mathematics since day one. “I can’t approach a match thinking about winning, losing or drawing. I only know how to prepare to win, win and win," De la Fuente insisted. "To reach the final you have to play against all the great teams. "
Article Summary: Norway manager Stale Solbakken gambled on rest, sacrificing a match to prepare for crucial knockout stages. Portugal manager Roberto Martínez would have stressed over finishing first in his World Cup group. Spain boss Luis de la Fuente has completely ignored the bracket mathematics since day one.

Article Title: Russia set for return as FIFA announces huge step to lift international football ban
Article Text:
FILE - FIFA President Gianni Infantino follows an international friendly soccer match between Iran and Costa Rica, in Antalya, southern Turkey, Tuesday, March 31, 2026. (AP Photo/Riza Ozel, File)

Why Russia was banned from international football

FIFA invites all member associations to new Under-15 tournament

Infantino wants Russia back in international football

Russia welcomes FIFA's latest decision

Senior return remains uncertain

Russia could be edging closer to a return to FIFA competitions after football's governing body confirmed that all 211 member associations have been invited to the inaugural FIFA Under-15 World Cup and Festival in Azerbaijan later this year. The move represents the clearest sign yet that FIFA is beginning to reintegrate Russian teams after more than four years of exclusion following the country's invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.While Russia's senior men's and women's national teams remain suspended from FIFA and UEFA competitions, FIFA President Gianni Infantino has also indicated that the governing body is prepared to reconsider the broader ban, arguing that continued isolation has failed to achieve its intended objectives.FIFA and UEFA jointly suspended all Russian national and club teams from official competitions in February 2022 following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.The decision immediately ruled Russia out of the 2022 FIFA World Cup play-offs and has since kept the country out of every major senior international competition, including UEFA Euro 2024, the UEFA Nations League and qualification for the ongoing 2026 FIFA World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico.The suspension also extended beyond the senior sides, with Russian youth and women's teams disappearing from FIFA and UEFA competitions. Russian clubs were likewise excluded from continental tournaments.Despite those sanctions, Russia's FIFA membership itself has never been suspended, meaning the country has remained one of FIFA's 211 member associations throughout the ban.During the past four years, Russia has continued to play international friendlies, primarily against nations outside Europe, including Trinidad and Tobago and Burkina Faso during the most recent international window.The first significant step towards Russia's return could arrive in October when FIFA stages its inaugural Under-15 World Cup and Festival in Azerbaijan between October 22 and October 31.In announcing the competition, FIFA confirmed that every member association has been invited."The first edition will be open to boys' teams from all FIFA member Associations, the second instalment in 2027 will feature girls' teams only," FIFA said."From 2028 onwards, all member associations will be invited to participate with both their boys' and girls' U-15 teams in two separate competitions."Although FIFA did not explicitly single out Russia in its announcement, reports from Reuters, DPA International and other outlets indicate that both Russia and Belarus are included among the invited participants.If confirmed, it would mark the first time since 2022 that a Russian team has competed at a FIFA-organised tournament.The move follows FIFA's gradual attempts to reintegrate youth football. The governing body had previously lifted the suspension affecting Russia's Under-17 boys' and girls' teams, although those sides have remained absent from UEFA and FIFA youth competitions after several European football associations, including Ukraine and England, continued to boycott matches involving Russian teams.Earlier this year, FIFA President Gianni Infantino openly acknowledged that the organisation is prepared to review Russia's suspension."We have to, because this ban has not achieved anything, it has just created more frustration and hatred," Infantino said."Having girls and boys from Russia being able to play football games in other parts of Europe would help."His comments represented the strongest indication yet that FIFA is exploring ways to gradually restore Russian participation, beginning with youth football before addressing the future of senior national teams.Russia's Sports Minister, Mikhail Degtyarev, welcomed FIFA's invitation to the Under-15 tournament, describing it as an important breakthrough after more than four years on the sidelines."We welcome FIFA's decision to allow the Russian national team to take part in the World Cup and the FIFA U-15 Festival, which will be held in Azerbaijan in October 2026," he wrote on social media."This is an important step toward bringing Russian teams back into international sport."For Russia, the invitation represents the country's first pathway back to a FIFA event since hosting the senior men's World Cup in 2018.Despite the development, Russia's senior national teams remain suspended indefinitely from FIFA and UEFA competitions.No timetable has been announced for lifting those sanctions, and Russia remains excluded from official senior international football while the war in Ukraine continues.The invitation to Azerbaijan therefore represents only a limited return through youth football rather than a full reinstatement.
Article Summary: FIFA invites all 211 member associations to new Under-15 tournament in Azerbaijan. Move represents the clearest sign yet that FIFA is beginning to reintegrate Russian teams. Russia's senior men's and women's national teams remain suspended from FIFA and UEFA competitions.

Article Title: Senegal vs Iraq FIFA World Cup match result: Senegal thrash 10-man Iraq 5-0 to keep their knockout hopes alive
Article Text:
Senegal achieved a historic 5-0 victory over ten-man Iraq, securing their World Cup knockout stage hopes. (AP Photo)

When Senegal needed it most, they delivered in the most spectacular fashion imaginable. A dominant 5-0 dismantling of ten-man Iraq at Toronto Stadium on Saturday not only kept the Lions of Teranga's World Cup knockout hopes alive but it also made history, becoming the biggest ever win by an African nation at a FIFA World Cup.Senegal started with a purpose that their first two games had lacked. Within just four minutes, the tone was set. Abdoulaye Seck's whipped delivery from the right found Habib Diarra rising highest at the back post, his header looping powerfully into the corner to give Aliou Cissé's side the perfect start. Iraq were all over the place defensively, and things went from bad to catastrophic in the 13th minute as Rebin Sulaka dragged down a clean-through Sadio Mane just outside the box and, after a VAR review upgraded the yellow card to a red, Iraq were down to ten men with over 75 minutes still to play.Against the odds, the ten-man Mesopotamia Lions dug in bravely for the rest of the first half. A rejig in their defensive structure and a superb save from substitute goalkeeper Jalal Hassan, who came on at the break and kept the score to 1-0 at half-time, and for a moment it looked like Senegal might be frustrated.The second half told a completely different story. Senegal simply turned on the power. Crystal Palace winger Ismaila Sarr tapped home coolly from Lamine Camara's precise cut-back in the 56th minute to double the lead, and just 89 seconds after coming off the bench, Pape Gueye curled an absolute beauty into the far corner with his very first touch.Gueye then added a stunning second in the 71st minute, a thunderous strike clocked at 132km per hour that left Hassan with no chance whatsoever.Iliman Ndiaye put the icing on the cake in the 82nd minute, driving in from wide on the right before unleashing a vicious long-range effort that rocketed into the net, completing a rout that reverberated across the entire African continent.As things stand, Senegal sit fifth in the best third-place standings and are on course to face England in the round of 32. Iraq bow out of their first World Cup since 1986 with nothing to show.
Article Summary: Senegal defeated Iraq 5-0 in their World Cup Group H clash. It is the biggest ever win by an African nation at a FIFA World Cup. Iraq were reduced to 10 men after Rebin Sulaka was sent off in the second half.

Article Title: France vs Norway FIFA World Cup match result: Dembele masterclass steers France past Norway as they seal Group I in style
Article Text:
In an electrifying first half, Ousmane Dembele scored a hat-trick, guiding France to a decisive 4-1 victory over Norway. (AP Photo)

Reigning Ballon d’Or winner Ousmane Dembele put on a breathtaking first-half masterclass, netting a sensational hat-trick to power France to a commanding 4-1 victory over Norway at the Gillette Stadium on Friday. With this dominant win, Les Bleus maintained their perfect record to top Group I, while Norway advanced to the Round of 32 as group runners-up.The match began at a frantic pace, with French captain Kylian Mbappe rattling the crossbar just 21 seconds into the game after an explosive sprint up the right wing. France did not have to wait long for the breakthrough, as Dembélé opened the account in the seventh minute, latching onto a precise pass from Mbappé and blasting a right-footed strike past Norwegian goalkeeper Egil Selvik.Dembélé doubled the cushion in the 20th minute, weaving between three defenders at the edge of the box before unleashing a brilliant left-footed drive. In a stunning response, Norway struck back just 14 seconds after the restart when midfielder Thelo Aasgaard found the back of the net to make it 2-1. However, Dembélé crushed the brief Norwegian resurgence in the 32nd minute, restoring the two-goal cushion to complete the first first-half World Cup hat-trick since Russia's Oleg Salenko in 1994.Norway rested ten regular starters for this fixture, most notably leaving talismanic striker Erling Haaland on the bench for the entire ninety minutes to preserve him for the knockouts. Selvik started in goal in place of Orjan Nyland as part of the wholesale changes.Despite the structural shifts, Norway earned a massive lifeline in the 49th minute when Theo Hernandez tripped Oscar Bobb inside the penalty area. However, Jorgen Strand Larsen’s penalty was expertly batted away by Mike Maignan. Youngster Bobb remained a constant threat in the second half, but a solid French defense stood firm.Dembélé left the pitch to a standing ovation in the 65th minute, replaced by Bradley Barcola. The Paris Saint-Germain substitutes combined beautifully in second-half injury time, with Barcola turning provider for Desire Doue, who headed home France's final goal to cap off a historic evening in Foxborough.
Article Summary: Reigning Ballon d'Or winner Ousmane Dembele scored a hat-trick to power France to a commanding 4-1 victory over Norway at the Gillette Stadium on Friday. Les Bleus maintained their perfect record to top Group I, while Norway advanced to the Round of 32 as group runners-up.

He rushed for 2,000 yards, won a Super Bowl, became the highest-paid running back in NFL history, and then got down on one knee. Saquon Barkley 's 2024 season was, by any measure, one of the most complete years any running back has ever had, and his finances followed suit.His fiancée Anna Congdon has been present for all of it since before any of it started. She was there at Penn State when he was a star prospect, there when their daughter Jada was born two days before the 2018 NFL Draft, there through the injuries, the Giants chapter, and the move to Philadelphia that changed everything. She has built her own financial identity in parallel, as a social media influencer, model, and brand partner, without relying on Barkley's spotlight to carry her.Barkley's net worth is approximately $32 million in 2026. He has earned $79,942,750 from NFL contracts across his career and pulls in roughly $10 million annually from endorsements with Nike, Pepsi, and Toyota. Congdon's net worth is estimated at around $300,000, built through social media influencing, modeling, and brand partnerships including Savage X Fenty. As a household, they are sitting comfortably north of $32 million combined.Barkley entered the NFL with a philosophy that most 21-year-olds do not have. Before he signed his first professional contract, he told ESPN exactly how he planned to handle the money:That approach has compounded into a $32 million net worth over eight NFL seasons.The New York Giants selected Barkley with the second overall pick in the 2018 NFL Draft. His four-year rookie deal was worth $31,194,751, fully guaranteed, with an average salary of $7,798,688 and a signing bonus of $20,767,092. The Giants picked up his fifth-year option in April 2021. In 2023, they tagged him with a non-exclusive franchise tag, which was tweaked to include a $2 million signing bonus plus incentives, bringing his 2023 earnings to $10,091,000. That was his final year in New York.The Giants declined to tag him again in 2024, and Barkley hit free agency. He signed a three-year, $37.75 million deal with the Philadelphia Eagles in March 2024. His average annual salary was $12.6 million, with a $26 million signing guarantee and a cap hit of just $3.9 million in year one.What followed was one of the greatest single seasons in NFL history for a running back. Barkley rushed for over 2,000 yards, led Philadelphia to the NFC Championship, and won Super Bowl LIX. Eagles general manager Howie Roseman told ESPN:The Eagles rewarded Barkley with a two-year, $41.2 million extension after the Super Bowl win, making him the highest-paid running back in the league. He earns approximately $20.6 million per year, with $36 million in guaranteed money.Here is a full breakdown of Barkley's year-by-year NFL earnings:$79,942,750Barkley earns approximately $10 million per year from endorsements with Nike, Pepsi, and Toyota, according to sports marketing analyst Bob Dorfman speaking to Front Office Sports.His Nike partnership is the headline. On "Sneaker Shopping" with Complex, Barkley spoke about how the deal extends well beyond just him:He also collaborated with Travis Scott on a signature Nike shoe. Barkley credited Scott directly:Scott's creative fingerprint shows up in a specific detail: the eye black design on the shoe was entirely his idea.Barkley also landed on the cover of Madden 2026 with a 99 player rating, a milestone he described as a childhood dream realized.On the investment side, Barkley has gone well beyond what most athletes attempt. He has invested $13.2 million across the S&P 500 and more than 10 startups, including AI company Anthropic, Elon Musk's neurotech firm Neuralink, prediction market Polymarket, and Peter Thiel's Founders Fund venture capital vehicle. Individual checks reportedly ranged from $250,000 to $500,000 per company, according to a report by The Profile.He invested in Bitcoin through mobile payments app Strike and announced plans to convert all marketing and endorsement income to Bitcoin via the app. Since making that move, Bitcoin's price has more than tripled, turning a $10 million income stream into a $35 million asset position, per The Profile.His framework for choosing where to put money reflects the same selectivity he applies to brand deals.Barkley said.He has also spoken openly about the ACL injury he suffered in 2020 as a turning point in how he thinks about money.he said on "The Best Business Show" podcast. Running backs tend to have shorter careers than most NFL positions, and that awareness pushed him to build financial infrastructure that outlasts football.Barkley's most personal real estate move had nothing to do with square footage or market value.In 2018, the same year he was drafted, he bought a house for his mother. He had been promising her since he was two or three years old that he would do exactly that.he told ESPN.His own portfolio has since grown in a measured, deliberate way.Barkley purchased a $3.9 million home in Malvern, Pa., after signing with the Eagles. The location places him close to the team's NovaCare Complex and near the community where he grew up in the Lehigh Valley. He also owns a home in Whitehall, Pa., close to where he starred at Whitehall High School, which is now valued at $728,000 according to Redfin. His former North Jersey home sold for $3.1 million in July 2024, shortly after he left the Giants to join Philadelphia.The story starts at a Pi Kappa Phi party at Penn State in December 2016. Congdon, a freshman at the time, shared a photo of herself and Barkley and wrote:They made it official in January 2017.Two years later, life moved fast. On April 24, 2018, two days before the NFL Draft, their daughter Jada Clare Barkley was born. Barkley was standing on the Draft red carpet when he processed all of it out loud.In September 2022, the family grew. Their son, Saquon "SJ" Barkley Jr., arrived. Barkley described him simply:In February 2025, Barkley confirmed to People magazine that he and Congdon were engaged. They have been together since January 2017, nearly nine years as of 2026. They are not yet married.Congdon has been cited by Barkley as a driving force behind his decision to finish his college degree years after entering the NFL Draft. She pushed him to complete it, and he did. That detail says more about their dynamic than any red carpet photo could.The Barkley family lives in Malvern, Pa., in a home that keeps them grounded near where Saquon grew up and close to the Eagles' facility. It is not the kind of lifestyle that makes headlines for excess. Barkley does not flaunt wealth, and Congdon's social media presence reflects the same tone, family first, fashion second, authenticity throughout.Congdon is the face that shows up at Eagles games, at courtside NBA seats, and at events like the US Open, where she and Barkley were photographed at Serena Williams' final match in September 2022. She has been Barkley's constant through eight NFL seasons, two teams, a torn ACL, a Super Bowl ring, and an engagement.On her Instagram, which has surpassed 100,000 followers, Congdon posts lifestyle content, fashion, and family moments. She keeps her feed selective, low volume, high engagement, which makes her a more credible brand partner than influencers who oversaturate their audience with ads. Her partnership with Savage X Fenty and her work with VSCO fit that image: real, grounded, and consistent.Barkley, for his part, spends the offseason close to home. He fulfills his endorsement commitments, shows up for Nike campaigns and Pepsi shoots, and by all accounts is precisely the kind of athlete brands dream about: marketable without being manufactured, visible without being reckless.When their daughter Jada celebrated her father posting on Instagram about her birth back in 2018, Barkley had written:Eight years later, that is still the version of Saquon Barkley that shows up. On the field, he is the most explosive running back of his generation. Off it, he is a father who promised his mother a house before he could barely talk, and kept that promise the first chance he got.Anna Congdon was there for both.
Article Summary: Saquon Barkley and Anna Congdon have built a family while Barkley’s NFL career has moved from Penn State to the New York Giants and Philadelphia Eagles. Barkley's net worth is estimated to be in the low six figures. Barkley has $13.2 million in endorsements and $4.9 million in real estate.

Article Title: Shocking details emerge on how Detroit Lions star went from sleeping on the floor to a $112M NFL fortune
Article Text:
Shocking details emerge on how Detroit Lions star went from sleeping on the floor to a $112M NFL fortune(Getty)

Penei Sewell’s childhood struggle in American Samoa shaped the Detroit Lions star

How Penei Sewell turned those struggles into a $112M Detroit Lions future?

The Detroit Lions have built one of the NFL ’s toughest offensive lines around Penei Sewell, but the story behind his rise still hits harder. Now Sewell stands as one of the league’s highest-paid offensive linemen. But before the Pro Bowls, the contract extension, and his rise into Detroit’s leadership circle, Sewell’s life looked nothing like it does now. Years before becoming an NFL star, he slept on the floor of a tiny shack in American Samoa, living through the kind of struggle that shaped everything that followed.When the Detroit Lions drafted Penei Sewell with the seventh overall pick in April 2021, the league saw a generational offensive line prospect. But as MLive’s Kyle Meinke reported during that draft week, Sewell’s football story began in Malaeimi, American Samoa, where his family lived in a small shack by the beach with limited space and even fewer resources.The report detailed how Sewell grew up sharing that cramped home with his parents and siblings, often sleeping on the floor while his father, Gabriel Sewell, taught his sons football in the simplest way possible.Instead of real training tools, the family used two-liter bottles filled with rocks to build strength and technique.Also, Sewell lived with his parents and four siblings, and when relatives temporarily moved in, there were sometimes 11-12 people living in the same room.Sewell reflected on that life during his post-draft media availability in April 2021 after hearing his name called by Detroit.“We all stayed in a shack there and it was, like you said, a beach,” Sewell said. “It was really small, life was real simple. To come from that to where I am at today, it’s nothing but a blessing.”Per CBS Detroit’s draft-night coverage in 2021, Sewell also made it clear that football was never just about him.“Now look at us,” Sewell said. “I’m in a position to really change my family dynamic.”That dream started becoming real after the family moved to Utah, where Sewell quickly became one of the nation’s top offensive line recruits and eventually landed at Oregon.In Oregon, Sewell’s rise became impossible to ignore. Per MLive’s 2021 feature, coach Mario Cristobal knew almost immediately he was coaching someone different. Cristobal recalled that just days into camp, veteran offensive lineman Shane Lemieux walked into his office with a simple request.“Coach, I want to play beside Penei Sewell,” Cristobal remembered Lemieux saying.Cristobal said that moment confirmed what the staff had already started to believe.“We knew we had something special on our hands.”According to Pro Football, Sewell allowed just one sack in 1,376 career snaps at Oregon and won the Outland Trophy in 2019 as college football’s top lineman. By the time Detroit drafted him, he was seen as one of the cleanest offensive tackle prospects in years.That trust paid off in 2024, when the Lions handed Sewell a four-year, $112 million contract extension, which made him the highest-paid offensive lineman in NFL history at the time of signing. Two years later, Sewell remains one of Detroit’s biggest long-term investments.For the Detroit Lions, Penei Sewell is much more than a star tackle. He is proof that one of football’s biggest success stories started in the smallest of places.
Article Summary: Penei Sewell was drafted by the Detroit Lions with the seventh overall pick in April 2021. Sewell grew up in a small shack in American Samoa with his parents and four siblings. The family used two-liter bottles filled with rocks to build strength and technique. In 2024, the Lions handed Sewell a four-year, $112 million contract extension.

Article Title: San Francisco 49ers star Brandon Aiyuk sparks buzz with shocking livestream admission amid legal trouble
Article Text:
San Francisco 49ers star Brandon Aiyuk sparks buzz with shocking livestream admission amid legal trouble (Getty Images)

Brandon Aiyuk’s livestream comments bring fresh attention to his speeding case

Brandon Aiyuk’s split from his agent and growing 49ers tension paint a bigger picture

Brandon Aiyuk’s offseason keeps getting messier, and this time the spotlight has shifted away from football. The San Francisco 49ers wide receiver stirred fresh buzz after speaking about his legal situation during a livestream, bringing new attention to an active arrest warrant tied to a speeding case in California.The current situation adds another layer to what has already been a chaotic stretch for Aiyuk. Trade rumors have followed him for months, his relationship with the 49ers has looked shaky, and now his own comments have pushed his legal issue right back into the center of the conversation.Brandon Aiyuk’s latest viral moment came during a livestream where he addressed the legal issue directly. According to Total Pro Sports, the 49ers star admitted he may have an active arrest warrant and suggested he plans to stay away from California if authorities are looking for him.That instantly grabbed attention because the warrant is tied to an ongoing case. According to the report, the issue started after a viral YouTube video showed Brandon Aiyuk driving his Cadillac CT5-V Blackwing at high speed near Levi's Stadium. The footage reportedly showed him going over 100 mph in an area where the speed limit is 40 mph.Law enforcement later reviewed the video, confirmed Aiyuk owned the vehicle, and forwarded the case to the Santa Clara County District Attorney’s Office.That process led to a misdemeanour “exhibition of speed” charge and an arrest warrant.After the video spread online, Aiyuk addressed it himself and tried to calm things down.“My car content won’t come with speeding anymore,” Aiyuk wrote on YouTube.That response did little to stop the legal process, and now his own livestream comments have brought the story back into focus at a time when his football future already looks unclear.The legal issue is only one piece of a much bigger offseason story. Per reports, Brandon Aiyuk recently revealed on Instagram that he has not had an agent since November and has been handling everything himself.“I do not have an agent. I terminated my SRA with my previous agent through the NFLPA last year in November,” Aiyuk wrote.He followed that by making his position clear. “There’s nobody who will be speaking for me. You’re going to get it from me live and direct.”That helps explain why Aiyuk’s social media activity has become more aggressive in recent weeks. He has repeatedly posted messages that hint at frustration with the San Francisco 49ers, while also fueling fresh trade talk. One of those posts showed him holding a Washington Commanders football, which quickly drew attention because of his connection to former Arizona State teammate Jayden Daniels.At the same time, 49ers general manager John Lynch admitted it is “safe to say” Aiyuk may have already played his final game for the franchise.With contract questions, public tension, and legal trouble now all hitting at once, Brandon Aiyuk’s future with the San Francisco 49ers looks more uncertain than ever.
Article Summary: Brandon Aiyuk’s livestream comments bring fresh attention to his speeding case. The San Francisco 49ers star admitted he may have an active arrest warrant and suggested he plans to stay away from California if authorities are looking for him. According to a report, the issue started after a viral YouTube video showed Brandon AiyUK driving his Cadillac CT5-V Blackwing at high speed near Levi's Stadium.

Article Title: Dianna Russini went from top NFL reporter to persona non grata and analysts say there's no coming back
Article Text:
Dianna Russini. (Getty Images)

Will Dianna Russini ever work in NFL media again?

Was Dianna Russini's $800,000 salary actually worth it for The Athletic?

Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic in April after her undisclosed relationship with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel became public. Now, a New York Times investigation has pulled back the curtain on what went on behind the scenes during her tenure there, and the picture is not flattering. The fallout has industry analysts questioning not just her conduct, but the credibility of every story she broke over the past three years.Terry Biggs, an analyst at PFSN, is not optimistic. Dianna Russini spent years building a reputation as one of the most plugged-in reporters in the league. Accurate scoops, solid sourcing, the kind of credibility that gets you booked on every major show. That reputation is now in serious trouble."Russini will find a home at Barstool or Dan Le Batard's show. She will conduct a teary interview. However, her reputation inside the league is shot. She may become an NFL commentator, but this scandal is now her entire legacy," Biggs said.That last line carries real weight. Legacy, in sports media, is the sum of what you leave behind when the noise dies down. For Russini, the noise is not dying down. The New York Times report revealed that her contract with The Athletic was set to expire in June and was up for renewal when the scandal broke.The Athletic is still conducting an internal investigation into her journalistic standards since she joined the outlet from ESPN three years ago.The revelation of her relationship with Vrabel has also cast doubt on stories she broke during her time there. When a reporter's sourcing comes into question, every exclusive gets revisited. Fans and analysts have spent the offseason doing exactly that.The NYT piece also revealed that Dianna Russini was earning $800,000 annually at The Athletic. On the surface, that sounds significant. In context, according to Jared Stillman of the Stillman & Company show, it barely registers.Stillman broke down exactly how the NFL insider ecosystem functions, and why the numbers matter so much."The value of the insider is to have somebody perpetuate the rumor cycle so that shows like this one, and all of the shows on ESPN, and all of the others can run with it," Stillman said. "Let's say one of these insiders who has the credibility -- Rapoport, Schefter, Russini, Breer, Pelissero — let's say one of them puts out there that Joe Burrow is unhappy with the Bengals, may look for a trade at the end of the year. That's gonna blow up. ""Every show is gonna talk about it," he continued. "Every team, every city, what does our team need to do to get Joe Burrow? It's all about the clicks and the recognition and everything else."Stillman was blunt about how the sausage gets made: rumours fuel clicks, clicks fuel airtime, and the whole machine runs on credibility. Without it, an insider is just noise."It's a dirty game, it is what it is," Stillman added. "But you have to break the news at some point for people to think you have the credibility that you can also spread gossip. I say all of that because, again, I think those guys make millions. I think Schefter makes $9 million dollars a year. If Schefter's making $9 million, and Rapoport's making in the millions, and all of these guys are in the millions, $800,000 isn't really that big of a deal. "That framing changes the conversation. Dianna Russini was not the highest-paid voice in the room. She was, by that measure, a mid-tier insider with A-tier access -- access that has now been scrutinised in ways that go far beyond her paycheck. The Athletic's internal investigation will be the final word on what that access actually looked like.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic in April after her undisclosed relationship with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel became public. The fallout has industry analysts questioning not just her conduct, but the credibility of every story she broke over the past three years.

Article Title: "It's definitely hard": Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders star Ava Lahey makes candid confession as wedding plans collide with training camp
Article Text:
Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders veteran Ava Lahey is balancing the demands of training camp with planning her 2027 wedding to MMA fighter Tyger Vaniter. Entering her third season, Lahey credits strong time management and support from teammates for helping her manage both responsibilities. She also plans to include the DCC's iconic "Thunderstruck" wedding tradition while embracing a leadership role on the squad and mentoring incoming rookies.

Dallas Cowboys Cheerleader Ava Lahey embraces wedding planning during training camp

Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders sisterhood continues beyond the football field

The return of America's Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders has brought another busy summer for veteran cheerleader Ava Lahey. While preparing for one of the toughest training camps of her career, she is also planning her 2027 wedding to MMA fighter Tyger Vaniter. Rather than feeling overwhelmed, Lahey is relying on discipline, support from her teammates, and the time management skills that helped earn her place on one of the NFL's most recognized cheer squads.For many brides, planning a wedding is a full-time commitment. For Ava Lahey, it comes alongside long rehearsals, leadership duties, and the pressure of keeping her place on the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders roster.Now entering her third season with the team, Lahey says she has learned that organization is one of the most valuable qualities a DCC member can possess. Between practice sessions, appearances, and personal commitments, every hour counts.ID@undefinedCaption not available."It's a lot to balance for sure," she admitted while discussing her packed schedule.Still, Lahey believes the demands of being a Dallas Cowboys Cheerleader have prepared her well for this stage of life."Anyone who makes this team wouldn't make it if they weren't so good at time management and stuff like that," she explains. "That is one of the number one qualities you have to have to be on this team, is time management."Unlike many brides who carefully oversee every wedding detail, Lahey describes herself differently.She said she is a "type B bride" and admitted she is "not too particular" about many aspects of the planning process. That relaxed mindset has helped her avoid unnecessary stress while focusing on both her career and upcoming marriage.She also credits fellow cheerleaders who recently got married for helping her navigate the experience."Having teammates that have already been married and have recently gotten married, so I can ask anyone for advice or help on how they navigated this all, too," has been a huge help, she says.The close bond among Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders extends well beyond game days. According to Lahey, teammates continue supporting one another long after leaving the squad.ID@undefinedCaption not available.One tradition she is excited to include at her own wedding is the famous "Thunderstruck" dance, a performance many former and current DCC members share during teammates' wedding celebrations.Outside rehearsals, the friendships remain just as strong. Coffee meetups, Bible studies, and regular catchups help maintain those connections even after veterans retire from the squad."It's definitely a hard transition," she says. "Not that I've done it, but just seeing my friends go through that. So, it is being intentional about still seeing them, hanging out with them, being there for them."As one of the team's veterans, Lahey also hopes to become a mentor for incoming rookies. While she feels more confident after two successful seasons, she says earning a place on the squad is never guaranteed."Second year, you're coasting by, feeling a little bit more comfortable," she says. "And now, in my third year, I'm just really ready to pour into everyone else on my team, to start leading in a way that I feel comfortable and can. And helping everyone else out, especially the new rookies coming in, is what I really, really love to hone in on."Despite preparing for a wedding, Lahey remains focused on one goal above all else."We're never too comfortable, never safe — I'm still working hard for my spot," she adds.For Lahey, wedding planning may be a major milestone, but her commitment to the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders remains just as strong. As training camp unfolds, she continues proving that dedication, teamwork, and careful planning can make even the busiest chapter feel manageable.
Article Summary: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleader Ava Lahey is planning her 2027 wedding to MMA fighter Tyger Vaniter. Lahey credits strong time management and support from teammates for helping her manage both responsibilities. She also plans to include the DCC's iconic "Thunderstruck" wedding tradition.

Article Title: "That changed everything...": Gisele Bündchen breaks silence on the life she shared with Tom Brady after snubbing him on Father's Day
Article Text:
Gisele Bündchen breaks silence on the life she shared with Tom Brady after snubbing him on Father's Day.(Image via Getty Images)

Gisele Bündchen reflects on the life she shared with Tom Brady as she snubbed him on Father’s Day



Why did Gisele Bündchen ignore her ex husband Tom Brady on Father’s Day?



Tom Brady and Gisele Bündchen's marriage continues to dominate headlines even though it has been close to four years since they divorced one another. While Gisele Bündchen recently sparked outrage as she snubbed Tom Brady on Father’s Day, the Brazilian supermodel has now reflected on the life she shared with the NFL legend.A few days ago, the former Victoria’s Secret supermodel Gisele Bündchen reflected on her previous shoots with the W Magazine as she opened up about her life in Boston when Tom Brady was playing for the New England Patriots.Gisele Bündchen said, "I had moved to Boston and was living a much quieter life…”The Brazilian supermodel added, "Earlier in my career, everything was about achievement and saying yes to every opportunity. Over time, I began to understand the importance of finding balance."The Victoria’s Secret supermodel spoke about how she has learned to "become more present and more connected" with herself rather than "focusing on external expectations".She added, "That changed everything—not only my work, but also the way I experienced life."Gisele Bündchen ’s confession on the life she once shared with the seven time Super Bowl champion Tom Brady has raised eyebrows, considering how she snubbed him on Father’s Day.For the unversed, as the world celebrated Father’s Day, many fans were expecting the Brazilian supermodel to pay a tribute to her ex husband and father of her two children, Tom Brady.Instead, Gisele Bündchen sparked shockwaves when she made a heartfelt post wishing her new husband, Joaquim Valente, on Father’s Day.To make matters worse, she even posted several pictures of her children with Tom Brady alongside Joaquim Valente as she wished him.This made millions furious as many trolled Gisele Bündchen for ignoring the seven time Super Bowl champion Tom Brady.While Tom Brady has remained silent about the drama and has kept himself distant from it, he actually always made it a point to wish his ex wife Gisele Bündchen on Mother’s Day.Now, it seems the NFL legend has kept his focus on being there for his three children and focusing on his work.In the last few months, the NFL legend has spent a lot of time vacationing around the world with his children.He has often spoken about missing a lot of time with his children since he was always so busy in his career.It seems the seven time Super Bowl champion’s focus remains on spending the maximum time with his three children and staying away from serious relationships.
Article Summary: Gisele Bündchen recently sparked outrage as she snubbed Tom Brady on Father’s Day. The Brazilian supermodel has now reflected on the life she shared with the NFL legend. The NFL legend has kept his focus on being there for his three children and focusing on his work.

Article Title: "To get the trust back....": Travis Kelce in the middle of Taylor Swift and Blake Lively drama as new reports deepen Hollywood feud buzz
Article Text:
Taylor Swift's reported wedding to Travis Kelce has reignited speculation about her friendship with Blake Lively, who is reportedly not invited to the celebration. The reported absence follows months of tension linked to the It Ends With Us legal dispute, where private messages between the two surfaced in court filings. While neither Swift nor Lively has commented publicly, the reports have fueled fresh discussion about their once-close bond and its current status.

Taylor Swift wedding reports revive focus on Blake Lively friendship

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumors continue to build

The entertainment world continues to buzz about Taylor Swift ’s rumored wedding to Travis Kelce , but the latest gossip isn’t about the location or A-list attendees. Instead, the spotlight has turned to Blake Lively , who is not expected to attend the high-profile celebration, reports say. The move has sparked renewed speculation about the state of the long-time friendship, especially after months of public attention on legal battles that unexpectedly thrust Swift back into the spotlight.For years, Taylor Swift and Blake Lively were considered one of Hollywood's closest celebrity friendships. They appeared together at private gatherings, supported each other's careers, and shared a bond that seemed untouched by industry drama. That perception has changed dramatically over the past several months.Multiple reports now suggest Lively will not be part of Swift and Travis Kelce's reported wedding festivities in New York. While neither Swift nor Lively has publicly addressed the speculation, the reported absence has attracted attention because of their once-close relationship.One insider shared that the ladies "have spoken on the phone."They added, "Everyone around Taylor is aware that Blake could attend. She may not, but that won't be because she isn't welcome."Another source stated, "Blake is on the cusp of getting a second chance, and they have had some loose conversations to test the waters to get the trust back." The insider added that the "Gossip Girl" star "is slowly getting back into the fold now that Taylor is having a change of heart." One more source shared that the actress doesn't "want to be seen as not going to the wedding."The discussion had been building momentum following the legal battle surrounding It Ends With Us, which starred Justin Baldoni. Swift was not involved in the dispute but reports of the court proceedings indicate that private communications were made public. That unexpected press attention threw the singer into a controversy she otherwise had been able to avoid.One exchange stood out among the messages that emerged. Lively allegedly made an overture to reconnect, while Swift responded with words that many took as proof of emotional distance.She wrote, "I missed my 'funny, dark, normal-speaking friend' and described Lively's recent messages as feeling like a 'mass corporate email.'"The quote quickly spread across entertainment news and social media, with fans debating whether it reflected a friendship that had permanently changed.While speculation surrounding the guest list continues, attention also remains fixed on the scale of Swift and Kelce's reported celebration. Various reports claim the event could include an extensive guest list featuring celebrities from music, film, sports, and fashion. However, none of those details have been officially confirmed by the couple.Entertainment insiders have linked several well-known names to the rumored celebration, including Selena Gomez, Ed Sheeran, Gigi Hadid, Karlie Kloss, Zoë Kravitz, and Suki Waterhouse. Whether those reports prove accurate remains to be seen, but they have only increased public interest in the event.Against that backdrop, Blake Lively's reported absence has become one of the biggest talking points. Fans who once viewed Swift and Lively as inseparable are now questioning whether the friendship has quietly come to an end.Despite widespread speculation online, there has been no official confirmation of the guest list or explanation for the reported distance between the two stars. Until either Swift or Lively addresses the situation publicly, much of the discussion remains based on media reports and unnamed sources.The alleged wedding continues to grab worldwide attention right now as well as leaving unanswered questions about one of Hollywood’s most recognizable friendships. And for better or worse, the story has become almost as closely followed as the wedding itself.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift's reported wedding to Travis Kelce has reignited speculation about her friendship with Blake Lively. The reported absence follows months of tension linked to the It Ends With Us legal dispute. While neither Swift nor Lively has commented publicly, the reports have fueled fresh discussion about their once- close bond.
